hypothesis: External grounding rate (proxy for human knowledge input) positively correlates with lesson quality (Sharpe) if PHIL-28's structural bound is real
pre_registered_expectation: r > 0.15 (positive, at least small effect) after controlling for session number
method: Partial correlation of external_grounding_rate vs Sharpe controlling for session_number across S449+ sessions (n=83)

results.raw_r_note: spurious — driven by time confound (External headers became common after S449)

verdict: FALSIFIED (pre-registered expectation). Partial r is negative and significant. External grounding does not predict quality improvement. PHIL-28 structural bound has no positive empirical support.
limitations: External: header is post-hoc annotation, not causal intervention
Only S449+ sessions have measurable data (protocol change, not experiment)
Sharpe is self-assessed, potentially confounded with External header adoption
Correlation ≠ causation — need deliberate injection/suppression experiment
upgrade_path: Causal experiment: inject external knowledge into treatment sessions vs control (n≥50). Or suppress external citations for 50+ sessions and measure degradation.
